units.content: A scope is a query returning a set of coupling keys defining a subset of the universe
Scopes are computed on demand; they are URLs, not entities
Four seed types exist: seed_deps, seed_measurers, seed_properties, seed_crack_keys
Intersectional filters compose with AND: complexity_levels, inference_levels, time_window
ScopeDefinition.__post_init__ checks that exactly one seed_* field is populated
If more or fewer than one seed is populated, it is invalid
compute_dep_cone performs BFS on the dependency co-occurrence graph
Two deps are adjacent if they co-occur in any chain step's depends list
_measurer_chain retrieves the InferenceChain associated with a measurer entity
_chain_touches_cone checks if any step in a chain depends on a dep in the cone
_seed_keys_from_cone returns coupling keys whose measurer has a chain touching the cone
_scope_from_measurers collects seed deps from each measurer's chain, then computes the dep cone
_scope_from_properties identifies measurers associated with given properties
_parse_crack_key supports two formats: directed (measurer→target:property) and coupling (lo:hi:property)
_apply_filters filters coupling keys by complexity_levels, inference_levels, and time_window
compute_scope dispatches on the populated seed field and applies filters
compute_scope_size returns the count of coupling keys in the computed scope
